title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments. The mission lifted off on a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover accidentally pulled an entire rock off the surface while drilling a sample. Engineers spent days trying to reposition the arm and dislodge the rock before it finally detached.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The galaxy features reddish-brown dust clumps and filaments, with background galaxies visible through its outer regions.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis mapped changes in artificial light at night on Earth, highlighting areas where light pollution has increased or decreased. This study provides new insights into global urbanization and energy use patterns.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) to collaborate with industry for the Mars Telecommunications Network, aiming to provide reliable high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will support surface, orbital, and human exploration using high-performance Mars telecommunications orbiters.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to test cryogenic fuel management technologies in orbit, aiming to enable future in-space propellant depots for deep space missions.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: NASA astronauts and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot enjoyed fresh food delivered by Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station. The shipment included oranges, apples, onions, and other fresh produce.

title: Ice Moves Out of Aniak
summary: Ice melt along Alaska’s Kuskokwim River has led to ice jams and subsequent flooding. This natural event highlights the impacts of seasonal changes in Arctic regions.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is now being used by Branch Technology Inc. to create efficient and strong buildings on Earth. The company's Freeform 3D Printing process utilizes lightweight lattice structures for innovative construction.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew, including astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, met with congressional staff in Washington on May 12, 2026, showcasing the mission's zero gravity indicator 'Rise'. The nearly 10-day Artemis II mission is part of NASA's broader lunar exploration program.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) testing.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. This marked a significant expansion for the company following its strong performance in 2025.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX's CRS-34 mission successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt, following the docking of Progress MS-34 just over two weeks prior.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China are preparing for nine launches in the week of May 11, showcasing a busy period in space activities. The launches span multiple missions across both countries.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully conducted a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability, including deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion work, with Flight 12 approaching. The process follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Two SpaceX Falcon 9 launches and one Chinese Chang Zheng 7 launch are scheduled, potentially marking a calm period before an upcoming surge in spaceflight activity.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news item. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions in space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, which involves baking the parachute to ensure its functionality. This test is part of preparations for an upcoming space mission.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars landing by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 descent to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process, including an SME Forum in June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, preceded by online sessions in April and May.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and store data from the Moon. The initiative aims to capitalize on lunar exploration beyond traditional mining and infrastructure projects.

title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
summary: U-Space has selected Skynopy to support multiple missions using its ground-station-as-a-service model. Skynopy's approach is gaining significant traction in the space industry.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications and deep-space capabilities. The move aims to provide integrated and resilient communication solutions for customers.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This acquisition aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing as they face challenges in securing growth-stage capital. The region's space industry is struggling with limited late-stage funding compared to other regions.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program as inaccurate. The program leader stated that the CBO is not properly accounting for the technology being developed.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 per company, along with mentorship and support for defense market entry and fundraising.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined a preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, targeting a 2027 launch to test technologies in low Earth orbit before a planned Moon landing in 2028. The mission aims to validate capabilities critical for future lunar exploration.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company. This achievement introduces a new player in the space launch industry.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by its chief, Gen. Michael Guetlein, in March.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program disputed a Congressional Budget Office (CBO) cost estimate for the system, which is tied to President Donald Trump's proposal. The disagreement highlights differing perspectives on the program's financial requirements.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: Valour Consultancy predicts significant growth for the In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market in China and India. The report highlights the potential scale and expansion of these markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to provide data center services from space, following a $275 million Series B funding round. The company, previously rebranded, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data processing.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence within 12 months. This marks a significant step in enhancing Poland's space-based surveillance capabilities.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three largest U.S. mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to collaborate on direct-to-device (D2D) services, aiming to enhance competition in the sector.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines has acquired Goonhilly Earth Station, a U.K.-based provider of deep space communication services. This acquisition aims to enhance Intuitive Machines' capabilities in deep space communications.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is fully acquiring Aireon, a space-based air traffic surveillance company operating payloads on Iridium's network. The acquisition highlights opportunities in space-based VHF (Very High Frequency) technology.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues but saw significant growth in its space services revenue for the quarter ending March. The company's performance highlights a contrast between stagnant total earnings and expanding sector-specific income.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.
